title: Whole-brain Staining and Tissue Clearing Protocol of LINCS
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8vj29","text":"Here we present LINCS (Labeling Individual Neurons with Chemical dyes and controllable Sparseness), an ultrabright neuronal labeling system that achieves cell-type specific labeling with chemical dyes. We established and optimzed an efficient pipeline for rapid whole-mount staining procedure, integrating LINCS with tissue clearing and commercially available light-sheet microscopy.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":23,"length":1},{"style":"italic","offset":32,"length":1},{"style":"italic","offset":43,"length":1},{"style":"italic","offset":56,"length":1},{"style":"italic","offset":87,"length":1},{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":23,"length":1},{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":32,"length":1},{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":43,"length":1},{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":56,"length":1},{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":87,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"dfnak","text":"Tissue clearing is processed based on a modified iDISCO+ protocol. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2isbl","text":"Steps 5-9,11,14-16 are done with gentle shaking on a rocker (~5 rpm).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fnf92","text":"Steps 12-13 are done with gentle shaking on a shaker (~120 rpm).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8s0je","text":"Perform all steps in closed tubes (Eppendorf 5 mL tubes), ensuring they are fully filled to prevent oxidation.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"442o4","text":"It is recommended to cover marker labels on tube caps with transparent tape to prevent dissolution by organic reagents.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7s71n","text":"Keeping the samples in dark after step 11.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"873ps","text":"Samples should not be exposed to water after step completely dissolved before diluted in water; Sigma-Aldrich, V900418)","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fd7bh","text":"Heparin sodium (10 mg/mL stock)","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c8qs","text":"NaN3 (10% solution)(can be substituted with ProClin)","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":3,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"58e1h","text":"20%, 40%, 60%, 80%, 100% Methanol (v/v in ddH2O) ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":45,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7h7ng","text":"Dichloromethane (DCM, J\u0026K, 908525)","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dgjmg","text":"H2O2 (30% solution)","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":1,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":3,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1q7fm","text":"Alexa Fluor 647-conjugated monovalent Streptavidin (monovalent SA-AF647, Abcam, ab272190)","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7jbkl","text":"Dibenzyl ether (DBE, Sigma, 108014)","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Harvest the brains and post-fix in 4% PFA o/n at 4℃ and then for 1 h at RT.
Wash post-fixed samples with PBS for 3x30 min at RT.
Dehydrate samples at RT by incubating them in a graded methanol series (20%, 40%, 60%, 80%, v/v in ddH 2 O, 1h for each), followed by two 1 h incubations in 100% methanol.
Wash samples with PBS for 4x1 h at RT.

Wash sampels with PTx.5 first for 2x1 h at RT, and then o/n at 25–37℃ (the lower temperature is recommended for samples with labeled axons enriched in cortical regions; Adding 0.05% NaN 3 to PTx.5 is recommended for o/n washing).

Stain samples with monovalent SA-AF647 (1:100 diluted for bulk labeling; 1:250 diluted for sparse labeling) in PTx.5 supplemented with 0.05% NaN 3 for 3 days at 37–42℃ (37℃ is recommended for sparse labeling samples).
Rehydrate samples at RT by incubating them in a graded methanol series (80%, 60%, 40%, 20%, v/v in ddH 2 O, 1h for each), followed by two 1h incubations in PTx.2.
Pre-cool samples at 4℃ and then bleach samples in 5% H 2 O 2 (v/v in ice-cold methanol) at 4℃ o/n.
Wash samples with 100% methanol for 2x2 h at RT.
Delipidate samples in 66% DCM (v/v in methanol) at RT o/n.
Before imaging, gently invert the tube to ensure the solution is homogeneous. Note: It is recommended to perform imaging within one week.
Transfer samples to DBE in a new tube and store at RT until clear. The tube should be fully filled with DBE to prevent oxidation.
Delipidate samples in 66% DCM (v/v in methanol) for 3 h at RT. Wash samples for 2x15 min in 100% DCM.

Dehydrate samples at RT by incubating them in a graded methanol series (20%, 40%, 60%, 80%, v/v in ddH 2 O, 1h for each), followed by two 1 h incubations in 100% methanol. Note: Can stop optionally at this step o/n at RT.

Specific neuronal populations are labeled in vivo using seTurboID, delivered via AAV in Cre transgenic mice. Biotin is administered through drinking water. Three weeks after viral injection, brains are harvested and fixed in 4% paraformaldehyde (PFA). The tissue then undergoes pretreatment, whole-mount staining, and clearing procedures before light-sheet microscopy imaging.
Anesthetize the mouse and perform intracardial perfusion, first with ice-cold heparinized PBS (10 U/mL concentration), then with ice-cold 4% PFA, followed by ice-cold heparinized PBS.

AAVs is delivered to target regions at a rate of 23 or 46 nL/min using a Nanoliter 2020 Injector pump controlled by a Micro4 controller (WPI). Tissue samples are typically harvested 3 weeks post-injection to allow for sufficient transgene expression.

One week prior to sacrifice and perfusion, mice are administered biotin (2 mM) in their drinking water ad libitum . The biotin-supplemented water should be provided in sterilized bottles and replaced weekly. Note: Extending the administration period beyond one week does not affect the final labeling efficiency.
creator: Shilin Zhong
